name: Ray Santisi
description: American pianist, composer, arranger, college professor, recording artist (1933–2014)
abstract: Ray Santisi (1 February 1933 – 28 October 2014) was an American jazz pianist, composer, arranger, recording artist and educator. Santisi has played as a featured soloist with Charlie Parker, Stan Getz, Dexter Gordon, Mel Torme, Irene Kral, Herb Pomeroy and Natalie Cole.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Mel Torme with Herb Pomeroy Big Band and Gerry Mulligan/ Gerry Mulligan Sextet

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Galper
about_quotes.evidence: I went to Berklee... I had my teacher’s gig. J. Ray Santisi’s gig. And when I was going to that school in Copley Square I took a couple of lessons with Ray
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Galper interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 27, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Pat LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: all the stuff I had learned at Berklee and then with Herb Pomeroy and Ray Santisi, Charlie Mariano

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: Ray Santisi, a fellow piano players from Boston called it, “Mammaries from cats.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997
